insights.title: Gender Distribution
insights.insight: The workforce is predominantly male with 40.0% female employees. Gender diversity varies by department and should be monitored for equitable retention policies.

insights.title: Department Distribution
insights.insight: Research & Development employs the largest share (961 employees). Research & Development leads headcount. HR should tailor retention strategies by department workload and growth.

insights.title: Job Role Distribution
insights.insight: Sales Executive and Research Scientist are the most common roles. High-volume roles warrant targeted career development programs to reduce turnover.

insights.title: Age Distribution
insights.insight: Average employee age is 36.9 years with most employees between 30-45. Mid-career professionals represent the core workforce and key retention focus.

insights.title: Education Distribution
insights.insight: Bachelor's degree holders form the largest educated segment. Higher education correlates with specific roles in R&D and influences compensation expectations.

insights.title: Business Travel
insights.insight: Most employees (1043) travel rarely. Frequent travelers face higher burnout risk — a known attrition driver.

insights.title: Marital Status
insights.insight: Married employees represent the largest group. Work-life balance policies significantly impact this demographic's retention decisions.

insights.title: Monthly Income
insights.insight: Income ranges from $1,009 to $19,999 with median $4,919. Compensation equity reviews recommended for outlier roles.

insights.title: Years at Company
insights.insight: Many employees have fewer than 5 years tenure, indicating either growth hiring or early attrition. First 3 years are critical for retention investment.

insights.title: Performance Rating
insights.insight: 0.0% of employees report low performancerating (≤2). Addressing satisfaction gaps can directly reduce attrition risk.

insights.title: Work Life Balance
insights.insight: 28.8% of employees report low worklifebalance (≤2). Addressing satisfaction gaps can directly reduce attrition risk.

insights.title: Environment Satisfaction
insights.insight: 38.8% of employees report low environment satisfaction (≤2). Addressing satisfaction gaps can directly reduce attrition risk.

insights.title: Relationship Satisfaction
insights.insight: 39.4% of employees report low relationship satisfaction (≤2). Addressing satisfaction gaps can directly reduce attrition risk.

insights.title: Job Satisfaction
insights.insight: 38.7% of employees report low job satisfaction (≤2). Addressing satisfaction gaps can directly reduce attrition risk.

insights.title: Overtime
insights.insight: 28.3% of employees work overtime — one of the strongest predictors of attrition. Workload management initiatives should prioritize overtime reduction.

insights.title: Department vs Attrition
insights.insight: Highest attrition in 'Sales' at 20.6%. Targeted interventions recommended.

insights.title: Job Role vs Attrition
insights.insight: Highest attrition in 'Sales Representative' at 39.8%. Targeted interventions recommended.

insights.title: Education vs Attrition
insights.insight: Highest attrition in '1' at 18.2%. Targeted interventions recommended.

insights.title: Age Group vs Attrition
insights.insight: Highest attrition in '18-25' at 35.8%. Targeted interventions recommended.

insights.title: Salary Category vs Attrition
insights.insight: Highest attrition in 'Low' at 29.3%. Targeted interventions recommended.

insights.title: Correlation Matrix
insights.insight: Monthly Income correlates with Job Level and Total Working Years. Satisfaction metrics show inter-correlation, supporting composite satisfaction scoring.

insights.title: Department × Overtime Heatmap
insights.insight: Sales employees with overtime show elevated attrition rates. Cross-dimensional analysis reveals high-risk workforce segments.

insights.title: Feature Importance
insights.insight: Top attrition drivers include OverTime, MonthlyIncome, and Attrition. HR should prioritize these factors in retention strategy.
